name: nf4 / NormalFloat4
oneliner: Non-uniform 4-bit format with levels spaced at quantiles of a Gaussian — optimal for neural network weights, used by QLoRA/bitsandbytes.
explanation: Introduced in the QLoRA paper (Dettmers 2023). Neural network weights $\approx$ Gaussian when normalized. Uniform int4 wastes levels in sparse tails and under-resolves the dense center. nf4 places 16 levels at N(0,1) quantiles so each level covers equal probability mass (1/16). Reduces quantization error vs uniform int4 for the same 4-bit budget. With 'double quantization' (quantizing scale factors to fp8), effective ~4.127 bpw. Default in bitsandbytes load_in_4bit.
fundamentals: Lookup table: $\{-1.0, -0.6962, -0.5251, -0.3949, -0.2844, -0.1848, -0.0911, 0.0, 0.0796, 0.1609, 0.2461, 0.3379, 0.4407, 0.5626, 0.7230, 1.0\}$. Levels are denser near zero (spacing 0.08) and sparser in tails (spacing 0.28). Quantize: normalize per group $w_{\text{norm}} = w/\text{absmax}$, map to nearest level. Dequantize: $\text{nf4\_lookup}[\text{index}] \times \text{absmax}$.

foundational_papers.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized Language Models
